name: Baked Frankfurters and Rice (Usda)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350° F.
2. Line four 8x8-inch baking pans with heat-resistant freezer wrap, allowing enough extra wrap to fold over the top. Use one pan for each six servings or one-fourth of the recipe. Do not line pans if food is not to be frozen.
3. Place rice in a large baking pan. Add salt and water. Stir and cover. Bake 30 minutes. Remove from oven and let stand for 5 minutes.
4. Combine cooked rice with remaining ingredients: frankfurters, canned tomatoes, sweet pepper flakes, instant minced onion, and shredded cheddar cheese.
5. Pour one-fourth of the mixture into each 8x8-inch baking pan. Pack food tightly to avoid air pockets.
6. To Serve Without Freezing:
- Preheat oven to 400° F. Bake for 45 minutes or until pepper flakes and onion are tender.
7. To Freeze:
- Cool for 30 minutes at room temperature.
- Complete wrapping by pulling paper up over the top of the food. Fold edges together several times so the paper lies directly on top of the food, then fold ends of freezer wrap over the top and seal with freezer tape.
- Label with the name of the food, date of freezing, and last date for best eating quality (about 6 months). Freeze for 10 to 12 hours before removing packages from pans.
8. To Heat Frozen Food:
- Preheat oven to 400° F.
- Remove freezer wrap and place food in a baking pan.
- Bake for 1 1/4 hours or until the center is hot.
